summary: 10 concrete product mentions surfaced from 28 scraped HN stories.

items.name: Gemma 4
items.source_story_title: Local AI needs to be the norm
items.summary: Google’s open-weight Gemma 4 model family for local inference.
items.why_included: Commenters said it was fast enough and useful for private local search tasks.
items.evidence: One commenter said they were using gemma4 locally on an MBP M2 and it felt really good; another called Gemma4 crazy well trained for its size.

items.name: Lakebase
items.source_story_title: Lakebase architecture delivers faster Postgres writes
items.summary: Databricks’ managed Postgres product with separated storage and compute.
items.why_included: The thread treated the architecture as solid and compared it to familiar transactional databases.
items.evidence: Commenters said Lakebase is for transactional use cases, compared it to AWS Aurora, and noted Databricks customers use it for many small Postgres instances.

items.name: Forgejo
items.summary: Self-hosted Git forge for repositories, issues, and collaboration.
items.why_included: People praised it as a responsive GitHub alternative and said they never looked back.
items.evidence: A commenter said they had been running a self-hosted Forgejo, found it extremely responsive, and were really happy with it; another said they installed Forgejo at home and never looked back.

items.name: OneDev
items.summary: Self-hosted DevOps platform with repo hosting, CI, and issue tracking.
items.why_included: It got unusually strong praise as a forge people should not overlook.
items.evidence: One commenter called OneDev a fantastic tool and said people should really consider it; another noted the OSS version and enterprise plugins, including a web terminal for CI/CD debugging.

items.name: OrcaSlicer
items.source_story_title: Louis Rossmann offers to pay legal fees for a threatened OrcaSlicer developer
items.summary: Open-source 3D-printing slicer for Bambu and other printers.
items.why_included: The Bambu legal-drama thread repeatedly recommended it as the slicer to use.
items.evidence: Comments said “Buy a bambu; use Orcaslicer,” and others noted that Bambu printers still work with OrcaSlicer in local/LAN modes or after downgrades.

items.name: ClearURLs
items.summary: Browser extension that strips tracking junk from shared URLs.
items.why_included: It came up as the clean fix while people argued about banning query strings.
items.evidence: Commenters explicitly recommended the ClearURLs addon and pointed to Firefox’s Copy Clean Link feature as a cleaner-link workflow.

items.name: Wander
items.source_story_title: Show HN: An index of indie web/blog indexes
items.summary: Indie web/blog index for discovering smaller curated sites.
items.why_included: Commenters said it was a good way to wander the independent web and bookmarked it.
items.evidence: In the indie-web-index thread, one comment linked Wander and said they really liked its take on discovery of smaller curated websites; another said they were happy to bookmark it.

items.name: Think Stats
items.summary: Allen Downey’s free statistics book for Python programmers.
items.why_included: People praised Downey’s books as clearer than the alternatives and recommended the whole series.
items.evidence: Commenters said Think Stats bailed them out of a stats class and pointed readers at Think Complexity, Think DSP, Think Bayes, and ThinkPython.

items.name: Bambu Lab A1 mini
items.source_story_title: Louis Rossmann offers to pay legal fees for a threatened OrcaSlicer developer
items.summary: Consumer 3D printer that commenters called a standout value and easy win.
items.why_included: Several commenters described it as the printer that made them stop tinkering and just print.
items.evidence: The thread had repeated praise for the A1 mini as the first printer people loved, the printer that made them try 3D printing, and a model they’d recommend as a tool rather than a hobby.

items.name: Prusa CORE One
items.source_story_title: Louis Rossmann offers to pay legal fees for a threatened OrcaSlicer developer
items.summary: Enclosed CoreXY printer that commenters found worry-free and well built.
items.why_included: People praised it as a reliable, user-friendly alternative with strong openness and service.
items.evidence: Commenters said the pre-assembled Core One worked great out of the box, had been worry-free, and was the printer they’d recommend for a user-friendly setup.
